questions.question: Explain the role of the Program Counter (PC) and the Instruction Register (IR) during the fetch-decode-execute cycle.
questions.modelAnswer: The PC holds the address of the next instruction to fetch, while the IR holds the current instruction being decoded and executed. After fetching, the PC increments to point to the next instruction.

questions.question: Describe the process of executing a typical data transfer instruction, such as 'LOAD R1, [1000]'.
questions.modelAnswer: 1. Fetch and decode the LOAD instruction. 2. Place address 1000 into MAR. 3. Read data from memory into MDR. 4. Transfer data from MDR to register R1.

questions.question: Compare a Hardwired Control Unit with a Microprogrammed Control Unit.
questions.modelAnswer: Hardwired control units use fast logic circuits but are inflexible. Microprogrammed units use slower microcode but offer easier modification and implementation of complex instructions.
